steps.title: Use the map comparison
steps.content: Hawthorne compares historical and biographical knowledge to a map: accurate and useful, but cold and lacking charm. His point is that historical figures are usually preserved as facts rather than vivid personalities.

steps.title: Reject literal or prescriptive claims
steps.content: Choice A invents advice about writing on little-known people. Choice C turns the reference to legends into a recommendation to record fiction. Choice D treats the map and painting comparison literally instead of as an analogy. The answer is B.
